content.content:
# Bento Grid CSS Technical Claims

## Core CSS Architecture

**Grid Structure:**
- "display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4, minmax(0, 1fr))" creates four equal tracks
- "minmax(0, 1fr) instead of bare 1fr prevents long unbroken content (URLs, code) from blowing out a track"
- "grid-auto-rows: 180px" establishes fixed row heights for predictable spanning behavior

**Cell Styling:**
- Border radius between 20–28px is essential; the document notes "under 16px it reads as a plain card grid"
- "padding: 24px" and "overflow: hidden" clip edge-to-edge media to rounded corners
- Single consistent gap (16px) across all cells sells the tray aesthetic

**Spacing & Layout:**
- "gap: 16px" maintains uniform gutters throughout
- "grid-auto-flow: dense" fills holes left by spanning cells but "decouples visual order from DOM order"

## Hierarchy & Spans

- "exactly one 2x2 anchor cell creates the hierarchy" via "grid-column: span 2; grid-row: span 2"
- Secondary cells use "grid-column: span 2" for wide layouts or single 1×1 spans for dense backfill

## Responsive Behavior

The document prescribes breakpoint resets:
- At 768px: "grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))"
- At 480px: "grid-template-columns: 1fr; grid-auto-rows: auto" with spans reset to "grid-column: auto; grid-row: auto"
- "Relax the fixed row unit on one-column layouts" to prevent silent clipping on mobile

## Accessibility Limitations

- "grid-auto-flow: dense and span juggling reorder cells visually while screen readers and keyboard focus follow DOM order," violating WCAG 2.4.3
- Text over imagery requires "4.5:1 check" per cell; a "bottom scrim, rgba(0,0,0,0.55) to transparent" fixes contrast failures

## When Not to Use

- Mobile-heavy traffic: "A 4-column bento collapses to 8 to 12 stacked cards, which is just a long, hierarchy-free scroll"
- Long-form content: "Cells force content down to a phrase, a number, or one screenshot"
- Equal-sized cells: "If every cell is the same size you have a plain card grid wearing a trendy name"

## Browser Support

"CSS Grid and grid-auto-flow: dense have shipped in every major browser since 2017" with no prefixes needed in 2026.
